title: box-sizing: border-box
description: The one CSS trick to learn early.

questions.prompt: With box-sizing: border-box, how wide is this element?
questions.explanation: border-box: padding and border are INCLUDED in the 200px.

questions.prompt: Why do developers often set `* { box-sizing: border-box; }`?
options: So width includes padding and border — sizes stay predictable
questions.explanation: Predictable widths — the model most people mentally expect.

questions.prompt: Apply border-box globally, the modern-recommended way.
questions.broken:
/* fix so every element uses border-box */

.box { padding: 20px; }
questions.explanation: `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }` is the common pattern; `* { box-sizing: border-box; }` also works.
border-box: total width stays at 100 — content shrinks.

questions.prompt: How wide is CONTENT in that box?
When you set width: 50%, box-sizing decides:
options: Whether 50% counts before or after padding/border
questions.explanation: border-box → 50% is the total; padding/border eat INTO it. content-box → 50% is content only, box grows.

questions.prompt: The main reason border-box became popular:
options: Predictable width — set 300px and it stays 300px
questions.explanation: Widths behave the way most beginners expect.

questions.prompt: Frameworks like Bootstrap and Tailwind assume:
questions.explanation: Modern CSS frameworks apply * { box-sizing: border-box; } in their reset.
